title: Thyroid Tracking
description: Follow TSH and related thyroid markers across visits with a longitudinal timeline and per-report reference ranges.

seo.title: Thyroid Lab Tracking with MedLens+
seo.description: Track TSH and thyroid panel results over time. Turn scattered thyroid lab PDFs into a timeline you'll actually review before visits.

persona: Adult on levothyroxine with annual thyroid panels

sections.heading: Your TSH was 2.1 last year and 4.8 this time…
paragraphs: Thyroid panels often include TSH, and sometimes free T4, free T3, and antibodies. Values shift with medications, pregnancy, other health changes, or simply over the course of care.
Without a timeline, comparing this year's TSH to last year's means digging through folders. MedLens+ places each result in chronological context so the shift is obvious at a glance.

sections.heading: Ranges that match your lab
paragraphs: TSH reference ranges vary between laboratories. We use the range printed on each uploaded report when flagging values, and warn when you're comparing results from different labs.
A flagged TSH is a signal to discuss with your clinician — not a label like hypothyroidism or hyperthyroidism.

sections.heading: Prep for endocrinology and primary care visits
paragraphs: Before your appointment, review your thyroid timeline and export a brief noting what changed since your last visit. Bring questions, not conclusions: "My TSH rose after the lab switch — should we retest?"
We never recommend levothyroxine dose changes or other treatment adjustments. Medication decisions are exclusively between you and your healthcare provider.

faq.question: Does MedLens+ diagnose thyroid disorders?
faq.answer: No. We organize thyroid panel results and show how values relate to the reference ranges on your reports. Diagnosis requires clinical evaluation by a qualified healthcare professional.

faq.question: Can I track thyroid antibodies over time?
faq.answer: Yes, when antibody results appear on your uploaded reports. We extract and timeline any thyroid-related markers present in supported PDF formats.

faq.question: What if I switched laboratories?
faq.answer: We display cross-lab cautions when your thyroid results come from different laboratories. Methods and ranges may differ — confirm significant changes with your clinician.
